explanations.10: The {1..10} syntax creates a range from 1 to 10, so this loop runs exactly 10 times.
explanations.15: The date +%s command returns the current time as a number of seconds since January 1, 1970; we store it in a variable so we can compare it later.
explanations.18: The $(( )) syntax lets you do math in bash; here we're subtracting the start time from the end time to get how many seconds passed.
